title: Baby Gear That Earns Its Place in a Small Condo
intro: You're navigating a 3-month-old in a space where every item has to pull its weight — visually and practically. These picks use natural materials, hold up through daily use, and won't look out of place in a considered home.

items.title: babybay Original Bedside Sleeper
items.note: Solid beechwood with an unchanged design made in Germany — the compact footprint is purpose-built for tight bedrooms and the natural wood finish ages honestly in a real home.

items.title: Baby Delight Beside Me Dreamer Organic Bassinet & Bedside Sleeper
items.note: The organic mocha colorway and mesh walls read clean rather than clinical, and the zip-down side panel keeps the bedside sleeper function practical without adding visual clutter.

items.title: Design Dua Organic Kapok Nursing Pillow
items.note: Filled with naturally harvested kapok fiber and covered in heavy canvas — it holds its shape over months of daily use and looks considered rather than clinical sitting on a sofa.

items.title: Moonboon Nursing Pillow
items.note: Buckwheat hull fill gives it a sculptural, adjustable quality that foam pillows lack, and the muted natural colorway fits a grown-up interior without a slipcover.

items.title: Makemake Organics Organic Burp Cloth Muslin — Ivory & Taupe
items.note: GOTS-certified muslin with a terry interior — the neutral ivory and taupe palette means these won't look out of place draped on a chair, and the double-layer construction holds up through repeated machine washing.

items.title: Solly Baby Wrap — Flax
items.note: The TENCEL Modal blend is notably softer and less bulky than cotton wraps, and the flax colorway is restrained enough to wear with regular clothes in a small apartment where everything is visible.

items.title: Ergobaby Aerloom FormaKnit Baby Carrier
items.note: A structured carrier for parents who find wraps fussy — the knit construction looks more considered than padded buckle carriers and holds its shape through everyday use without stretching out.

items.title: Piccalio Play Mat
items.note: GOTS-certified organic cotton over natural latex — the two-sided design and machine-washable waterproof cover mean it stays presentable long-term, and the muted palette reads as a floor accent rather than a toy.

items.title: Lorena Canals Washable Play Mat — Water Lily Olive
items.note: Woven cotton construction that doubles as a room rug — the olive colorway holds up visually as baby gear phases out and the mat stays in the space as a proper textile.

items.title: Wee Gallery Baby Tummy Time Gallery with Mirror
items.note: Organic cotton shell with swappable art card pockets — the high-contrast graphic design is genuinely good-looking flat on a floor or propped up, not the usual garish primary-color tummy time fare.

items.title: Stokke Flexi Bath with Newborn Support
items.note: Folds flat for storage behind a door — the translucent tub and clean Scandinavian form hold up better visually in a small bathroom than the typical molded plastic tubs, and the newborn insert extends its usable range.

items.title: Nest Designs Organic Cotton 9-Layer Hooded Baby Bath Towel
items.note: Nine-layer organic cotton construction that gets softer with washing rather than pilling — the restrained print options mean it hangs on a hook without looking like nursery decor.

warnings: Moonboon Nursing Pillow is priced in EUR on a European domain — US shipping costs and availability should be verified before recommending to a US audience.
The Solly Baby Wrap is linked via The Natural Baby Co. retailer rather than the brand's own site — verify this is the current Flax colorway and not a discontinued variant.

Baby Delight Heirloom Willow Wood Bassinet was dropped in favor of the Beside Me Dreamer; if a non-bedside option is needed, the Heirloom Willow is a reasonable fallback but shares the same price point.
Green Cradle bassinet link returned a 404 — no usable product data, excluded.
Colored Organics burp cloth link resolved to a category page, not a product page — excluded as it lacks a specific product URL.
Wee Gallery product data listed brand as 'Alaska' which appears to be a scraping error — verify brand attribution on the product page.

Home of Wool Wool Baby Play Mat ($261) was considered but dropped; the price-to-use case ratio is harder to justify over the Piccalio at $189 for a first-time parent audience, and the woocommerce extraction had some uncertainty.
OXO Tot Splash & Store Bathtub dropped in favor of Stokke — both are solid, but the OXO lacks the fold-flat storage advantage that matters in a small condo.
